inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 diluted EPS $3.15 on ~257M shares; net income $810M (10-K XBRL). Regulated utility owner earnings recovered through authorized ROE and rate-base growth.
inputs.dividend_source: Targeted 2026 annual dividend $2.14 per share (+5% vs $2.03 FY2025); 10-K Liquidity and Capital Resources
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence uses filing earnings per share as starting owner cash. Heavy growth capital spending ($3.1B+ annually 2026-2029) is recovered via rate-base mechanisms, not subtracted from owner cash in the base case.

growth_explanation.mechanism: Rate-base growth from $12.4B 2026-2029 construction plan (renewables, storage, gas, distribution) plus data-center load under executed electric service agreements; IPL moratorium limits near-term Iowa retail electric base-rate increases through September 2029.
growth_explanation.years_1_5: 6% owner-cash growth from authorized capital recovery and load growth
growth_explanation.source: 10-K FY2025 construction table; Q1 2026 EPS +5% YoY

bear.notes: Rate moratorium, tariff/capex overruns, higher interest expense on $11B long-term debt

base.notes: Execute renewables/gas/storage plan; data-center load ramps; dividend grows ~5%

bull.notes: Faster data-center interconnections; favorable Wisconsin/Iowa rate outcomes; credit spread compression

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Regulated rate-base assets on balance sheet; no hidden land NAV pattern

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / dormant royalty?
option_scan.evidence: Electric and gas utility; no mineral acreage option

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: All segments profitable; FY2025 net income $810M

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Data-center electric service agreements drive future rate-base additions; revenue recognized as load connects

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution / legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: Stable regulated dividend; no litigation recovery catalyst

option_scan.question: Embedded product option in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Renewables and storage already in regulated revenue path as projects enter service

items: Data-center load timing and individual customer rate approvals need quarterly monitoring
IPL retail electric base-rate moratorium through September 2029 caps Iowa upside

paths.label: Growth theory (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative
